name: Tricky Sam Nanton
abstract: Joe "Tricky Sam" Nanton (February 1, 1904 – July 20, 1946) was an American trombonist with the Duke Ellington Orchestra. A pioneer of the plunger mute, Nanton is notable for his use of the distinctive wah-wah effect.

works.t: Black, Brown & Beige: Lighter Attitude (Emancipation Proclamation)

Sophisticated Ladies: It Don't Mean a Thing

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions 'Tricky Sam' but the interviewee does not respond or provide any information about their relationship.

about_quotes.speaker: Mary Lou Williams
about_quotes.evidence: I played in the band with Tricky Sam, Bubber Miley, and Sonny Greer

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: And the whole band -- Duke has a few lines in it, and the whole band got a lot of film space -- Toby, Harry, Johnny, Barney, Freddie Jenkins, Cootie, Arthur Whetsol, Tricky Sam, and you, Lawrence...

about_quotes.speaker: Sonny Greer
about_quotes.evidence: Tricky replaced him... Bechet and Tricky and Bubba, every night was a jam session... Bubber and Tricky would turn it around... Tricky growled and Bubber growled

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: I met Johnny Hodges, Duke, Ben Webster, Barney Bigard, Tricky Sam [Nanton], Lawrence Brown, all of them.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Albany Leon "Barney" Bigard
about_quotes.evidence: I think Whetsol and Tricky and myself, Duke and Sonny and -- about all.
